highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.195 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a new environment variable `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS` that lets you disable mouse click, drag, and hover interactions in fullscreen mode while still allowing scroll — useful for distraction-free reading. It also fixes a bug where hook matchers with hyphenated identifiers like `mcp__brave-search` were incorrectly matching substrings instead of exact names, which could trigger unintended hooks.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.193 Released
highlights.summary: This release introduces `autoMode.classifyAllShell`, a new setting that routes all Bash and PowerShell commands through the auto-mode classifier — not just commands that look like arbitrary code execution — giving you finer control over what Claude runs automatically. It also now logs denial reasons in the transcript, shows them in toast notifications, and surfaces them in `/permissions` for easier debugging of blocked actions.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.191 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.191 adds `/rewind` support so you can resume a conversation from before you ran `/clear`, making it easier to recover context you thought was gone. It also fixes a jarring scroll-position bug where the view would jump to the bottom while you were reading earlier output during a streaming response.

highlights.title: Show HN: Recall – Local project memory for Claude Code
highlights.summary: Recall is an open-source tool that gives Claude Code persistent, local memory about your project so it doesn't lose context between sessions. It stores project-specific notes and summaries on your machine, meaning nothing is sent to external servers — a good fit for developers working on private or sensitive codebases.

highlights.title: The text in Claude Code's "Extended Thinking" output is not authentic
highlights.summary: This article argues that the visible "thinking" text shown during Claude Code's Extended Thinking mode is not a genuine window into the model's reasoning process, and developers should be cautious about treating it as such. It's a useful read for anyone relying on Extended Thinking output to debug or audit Claude's decision-making, as the displayed text may not accurately reflect what drives the final response.

highlights.title: From Zero to Claude Code in 19 Minutes (no code)
highlights.summary: This beginner-friendly YouTube tutorial walks through setting up and using Claude Code from scratch in under 20 minutes, with no prior coding experience required. It's a great starting point if you want a fast, no-nonsense introduction to what Claude Code can do before diving into more advanced features.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: A 30-minute video guide aimed at developers who want to move beyond the basics and get productive with Claude Code quickly. It covers core workflows and best practices in a structured format, making it a solid next step after completing a beginner tutorial.

highlights.title: Show HN: Smart model routing directly in Claude, Codex and Cursor
highlights.summary: Workweave's open-source router sits between your coding agent and the underlying models, automatically sending each request to the most suitable model based on task type — potentially reducing costs and improving response quality. It works with Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor, and can be run locally for full control over routing decisions.

tips_of_the_day: Use `/rewind` after accidentally running `/clear` — as of v2.1.191, Claude Code can restore your conversation to the state it was in before the clear, saving you from re-explaining your entire context.

When debugging why Claude Code denied a command, open `/permissions` and check the 'recent denials' section (added in v2.1.193) — it now shows the exact reason for each denial, making it much faster to tune your auto-mode settings.

If you use MCP servers or custom hooks with hyphenated names like `mcp__brave-search`, double-check your hook matchers after upgrading to v2.1.195 — the fix to exact-matching means any overly broad substring matchers you relied on will no longer fire as expected.
